# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kong configuration for API routes that proxies to microservices. Configure upstream services, routes, and plugins for a production API gateway.
# Category: api_gateway_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: api-gateway # the service should be named 'api-gateway' as Kong uses it to identify downstream services in routing rules and plugins configuration
spec:
---
# Define upstreams for our microservices - replace with actual endpoints of your instances. Note, you need at least 2 routes configured here due to Round Robin load balancing mechanism enabled by default on the API Gateway (upstream_route). You can create more if needed based off what's returned from calling an upstream service
---  
apiVersion: konghq.org/v1alpha1
kind: KongPlugin
metadata:
  name: jwt-rs256 # we need this plugin to add JWT authentication functionality, replace with actual required plugins if any exists in your environment or Kubernetes cluster (like 'jwt')
spec:
---   
apiVersion: konghq.org/v1alpha1
kind: KongRoute
metadata:
  name: api-gateway # the route should be named as a gateway API for routing rules and plugins configuration, replace with actual routes if necessary in your environment or Kubernetes cluster (like '/status', 'ping')  
spec:    
---   
apiVersion: konghq.org/v1alpha1 
kind: KongRoute  # This would be the first route we define for proxy requests to microservices, replace with actual routes if necessary in your environment or Kubernetes cluster (like '/users', 'products')  
spec:    
---   
apiVersion: konghq.org/v1alpha1 
kind: KongRoute # This would be the second route we define for proxy requests to microservices, replace with actual routes if necessary in your environment or Kubernetes cluster (like '/orders', 'inventory')  
spec   
... end of YAML code ...
